name: Camino Natural del Tajo and Jarama Canal Loop from Aranjuez
name_original: Camino Natural del Tajo y Canal de Jarama – circular desde Aranjuez
description: This difficult 44.3-mile mountain biking loop near Aranjuez follows the Tajo River and Jarama Canal, offering varied terrain and scenic view
region_intro: Mountain biking around Aranjuez offers diverse terrain shaped by the confluence of the Tagus and Jarama rivers. The region features a fertile valley with riparian forests, expansive royal gardens, and rolling agricultural lands. This landscape provides a variety of mountain bike trails, from paved paths within the town to unpaved gravel tracks in the surrounding countryside. The area is characterized by its unique blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage, making it suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Aranjuez The most popular mountain bike trail is Aranjuez – circular por la Laguna de Noblejas y el Palacio Real de Aranjuez, a 20.9 miles (33.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 43 minutes to complete, offering views of the Royal Palace and Laguna de Noblejas. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Camino Natural del Tajo and Jarama Canal Loop from Aranjuez, a difficult 44.3 miles (71.3 km) path. This route follows the Tagus River and Jarama Canal, featuring varied terrain and scenic views. Local mountain bikers also love the Castle of Oreja – Castillo de Oreja loop from Aranjuez, a 13.7 miles (22.0 km) trail leading through the rolling countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 38 minutes. Mountain biking around Aranjuez is defined by riparian forests along the Tagus and Jarama rivers, expansive royal gardens, and agricultural lands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Aranjuez are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Aranjuez's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Aranjuez?
A: There are over 45 mountain bike trails around Aranjuez, catering to various skill levels. This includes 10 easy routes, 30 moderate routes, and 5 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners to experienced riders.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Aranjuez?
A: The terrain in Aranjuez is diverse, shaped by the Tagus and Jarama rivers. You'll find a mix of paved paths within the town, unpaved gravel tracks in the surrounding countryside, and trails through riparian forests, agricultural lands, and rolling hills. This variety offers a dynamic riding experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Aranjuez?
A: Yes, Aranjuez offers several easy mountain bike trails suitable for families. These routes often feature flatter sections and scenic views, such as those found within the royal gardens or along the rivers. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40350703" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Oreja – Castillo de Oreja loop from Aranjuez&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 22 km ride.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Aranjuez?
A: Aranjuez is rich in natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can ride past the confluence of the Tagus and Jarama rivers, explore the extensive Royal Gardens like Jardín del Príncipe and Jardín de la Isla, and see the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4576777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Aranjuez&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also pass through agricultural lands and offer views of various lakes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2782939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Noblejas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Aranjuez?
A: Yes, many mountain bike trails in Aranjuez are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961669741/aranjuez-circular-por-la-laguna-de-noblejas-y-el-palacio-real-de-aranjuez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aranjuez – circular por la Laguna de Noblejas y el Palacio Real de Aranjuez&lt;/a&gt;, a 33.7 km trail offering views of the Royal Palace and Laguna de Noblejas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Aranjuez?
A: The milder seasons, such as spring and autumn, are generally the best time for mountain biking in Aranjuez. The weather is pleasant, and the natural landscapes, including the royal gardens and riparian forests, are particularly vibrant. Summer can be hot, while winter might be too cold for some, especially in the open countryside.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Aranjuez offers several difficult routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961678946/camino-natural-del-tajo-and-jarama-canal-loop-from-aranjuez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Natural del Tajo and Jarama Canal Loop from Aranjuez&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 71.3 km path that follows the Tagus River and Jarama Canal, featuring varied and technical terrain.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Aranjuez?
A: The mountain bike trails in Aranjuez are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 120 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse scenery, the blend of cultural heritage with natural beauty, and the variety of routes suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by historical sites in Aranjuez?
A: Yes, many routes in Aranjuez incorporate its rich history. You can find trails that offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4576777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Aranjuez&lt;/a&gt;, an 18th-century Baroque and Rococo palace. Other historical points of interest like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2601433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casasola Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2561736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Chapel of San Isidro&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible or visible from various routes.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the agricultural areas or vineyards around Aranjuez?
A: Yes, beyond the formal gardens, the surrounding countryside of Aranjuez features extensive agricultural lands, wineries, and olive groves. Many routes, particularly those venturing further from the town center, will take you through these scenic areas, offering a different perspective of the region's landscape and local produce.

Q: What is a good moderate mountain bike route to explore the area?
A: A great moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10374244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Aranjuez – Royal Palace of Aranjuez loop from Aranjuez&lt;/a&gt;. This 24.6 km route takes about 2 hours and 15 minutes, offering a balanced ride with scenic views and a good workout without being overly strenuous.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike loop is approximately 71.3 km (44.3 miles) long. On average, it takes about 5 hours and 18 minutes to complete, though this can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While some sections of the Camino Natural del Tajo are considered easy, this specific loop is rated as 'difficult' overall. It features varied terrain, including dirt, stones, and sandy sections, and requires good fitness. Beginners might find parts challenging, potentially needing to push their bike on some unpaved segments.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Camino Natural del Tajo and Jarama Canal Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter wide, comfortable tracks, but also sections with dirt, stones, and sand that can make progress more challenging. The route passes through agricultural fields, along riverbanks, and through olive groves, offering a diverse riding experience.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and natural features along this route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers picturesque views of the Tajo River as it winds through the landscape, alongside agricultural lands and large estates. Key highlights include a Beautiful section of the Camino Natural del Tajo (GR 113) and the historic Railway Bridge over the Jarama River. You'll also see areas historically influenced by the Tajo's water, contributing to the region's fertility, and pass near the historic Royal Site of Aranjuez.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Camino Natural del Tajo and Jarama Canal Loop from Aranjuez?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends in Aranjuez. You can typically find parking within the town, especially near the river or the Royal Site, which provides convenient access to the trailheads along the Tajo River.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to ride this loop. The weather is milder, making for more comfortable conditions. Summers can be very hot in this region, and while winters are usually mild, some sections might be muddy after rain.

faqs.question: Is the Camino Natural del Tajo and Jarama Canal Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on this trail. However, as parts of the route pass through agricultural areas and natural spaces, it's advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially to protect local wildlife and respect private property.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to ride this trail, especially within the ZEPA Carrizales y Sotos de Aranjuez?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to access the Camino Natural del Tajo or to ride this loop within the ZEPA Carrizales y Sotos de Aranjuez. However, as it's a protected natural area, please adhere to all environmental regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the Aranjuez area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop incorporates significant sections of the Camino Natural del Tajo (GR 113). It also briefly intersects with other paths such as MTB Madrid a Aranjuez, Ciclamadrid, Camino de los Pucheros, and Camino de Aranjuez o del Valle, offering potential for extended exploration.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or sections to be aware of on this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for varied terrain, including some sandy and stony sections that can be challenging, especially after dry periods. While the overall elevation gain is moderate, the length and diverse surfaces contribute to its 'difficult' rating. Ensure your mountain bike has good suspension and appropriate gearing for varied ascents.

waypoints.name: Beautiful section of the Camino Natural del Tajo (GR 113)
Railway Bridge over the Jarama River
